Course closed for this cycle: Asian and Middle Eastern Studies (Muslim-Jewish Relations) is no longer accepting applications for this cycle. It is expected to re-open for new applications in early September.
Sep. 3, 2025
Application deadline
May. 14, 2026
These deadlines apply to applications for courses starting in Michaelmas 2026, Lent 2027 and Easter 2027.
matchedText: Course closed for this cycle: Asian and Middle Eastern Studies (Muslim-Jewish Relations) is no longer accepting applications for this cycle. It is expected to re-open for new applications in early September.
matchedTextAfter: The Muslim-Jewish Relations pathway is designed for highly motivated students who would like to develop their knowledge and understanding of Muslim-Jewish relations, together with the research skills necessary to carry out independent research work to a high academic standard through engagement with primary sources in one or more Middle Eastern languages. Graduates will be well placed to pursue further research in a PhD programme, and/or to work in NGOs, charities, government, education, or any other field where cultural understanding, linguistic skills and high-level critical thinking, analysis and communication skills are needed.
